title: Microsoft strikes 'multibillion-dollar' deal with French AI firm Mistral
excerpt: Microsoft and French startup Mistral said Tuesday they had signed a "multibillion-dollar" deal for the US tech giant to use the AI developer's computing infrastructure in Europe.
content_html:
Microsoft has agreed to spend billions of dollars on Mistral’s computing infrastructure in Europe under a deal that will also expand distribution of the French AI startup’s technology through the US cloud and software giant, ​the companies said ‌on Tuesday.

As part of the agreement, Microsoft Azure customers will be able to develop software ⁠using Mistral’s data centres in France, giving Microsoft more capacity in Europe and regulated industries an alternative to US-controlled infrastructure.

Watch moreWhy Anthropic's most powerful AI models are back online

Mistral, meanwhile, has added its AI models called Medium 3.5 ‌and OCR 4 to Microsoft’s app builder known as Foundry. Microsoft Copilot Studio has brought on ⁠Medium 3.5 as well.

Finally, businesses with independent data centres that access Microsoft services via Azure Local will have the option to run Mistral’s “open” models, which give customers license to develop AI as their own.

The deal underscores ​growing interest in Europe and elsewhere to reduce dependence on US technology so other countries may ‌have greater say in their future society and economy. It may also help Microsoft meet rising demand for open models.

Read moreEU orders Google to share search data and open Android system to AI rivals

Though the push for “sovereign” AI is now years old, a US decision last month to pause foreign access to two advanced models from San Francisco-based Anthropic ‌has made technology independence a more urgent issue in Europe.

In a joint interview with Reuters, Microsoft President Brad Smith and Mistral CEO Arthur Mensch said the partnership aimed to deliver ​such sovereignty while allowing access to US software and security features.

“By putting Mistral’s models on Azure Local and on Mistral’s computational capacity, we can combine American and European technology and do it in a way that provides continuous and assured ​access,” Smith said.

The AI lab behind the Claude chatbot is also in talks for a deal worth billions to use infrastructure belonging to Facebook and Instagram parent Meta, according to media reports.

Staying in the AI race

Decoupling Europe from US technology would be a tall order. Nvidia chips powering the global ​AI boom, also key to Mistral’s data-center buildout, are American-designed.  Nvidia, like Microsoft, is a ​Mistral investor.

Watch moreCan Europe own its digital destiny? EU unveils tech sovereignty roadmap

Smith said the deal announced on Tuesday did not include any new financial stake in the startup, and Mensch declined to comment on a Bloomberg News report that ​Mistral was in talks to raise around €3 billion ($3.4 billion) at a €20 billion valuation.

To display this content from YouTube, you must enable advertisement tracking and audience measurement.

One of your browser extensions seems to be blocking the video player from loading. To watch this content, you may need to disable it on this site.

The Paris-based lab has come to represent one of Europe’s top hopes in AI. So far it has targeted manufacturing, financial services and defense sales and has won business from France’s armed forces. Its valuation remains dwarfed by US peers such as Anthropic.

Still, Mensch said the deal showed how Microsoft and Mistral were “working together ⁠on closing the gap on the infrastructure side in Europe”.

Mistral is targeting 1 gigawatt of compute capacity by 2030, and the Microsoft agreement – specifics of ⁠which Mensch declined to provide – ​validates its strategy.

The companies are working on a joint go-to-market plan, they added.

“This is going to help both of our companies grow our businesses, unquestionably," Smith said.

(FRANCE 24 with Reuters and AFP)

It may also help Microsoft meet rising demand for open models. sentences.en: Its valuation remains dwarfed by US peers such as Anthropic.
sentences.zh: 与 Anthropic 等美国同行相比，其估值仍然相形见绌。

blocks.html_en: Still, Mensch said the deal showed how Microsoft and Mistral were “working together ⁠on closing the gap on the infrastructure side in Europe”.

sentences.zh: 尽管如此，门施表示，这笔交易表明微软和米斯特拉尔如何“共同努力缩小欧洲基础设施方面的差距”。

blocks.html_en: Mistral is targeting 1 gigawatt of compute capacity by 2030, and the Microsoft agreement – specifics of ⁠which Mensch declined to provide – ​validates its strategy.

sentences.en: Mistral is targeting 1 gigawatt of compute capacity by 2030, and the Microsoft agreement – specifics of ⁠which Mensch declined to provide – ​validates its strategy.
sentences.zh: Mistral 的目标是到 2030 年实现 1 吉瓦的计算能力，而微软协议（Mensch 拒绝提供的具体细节）验证了其战略。

blocks.html_en: The companies are working on a joint go-to-market plan, they added.

blocks.html_en: “This is going to help both of our companies grow our businesses, unquestionably," Smith said.

sentences.en: “This is going to help both of our companies grow our businesses, unquestionably," Smith said.
sentences.zh: “毫无疑问，这将有助于我们两家公司发展业务，”史密斯说。

blocks.html_en: (FRANCE 24 with Reuters and AFP)

sentences.en: (FRANCE 24 with Reuters and AFP)
sentences.zh: （法国 24 小时电视台、路透社和法新社）

content_html_zh:
两家公司周二表示，微软已同意在欧洲的 Mistral 计算基础设施上投入数十亿美元，该协议还将通过这家美国云和软件巨头扩大这家法国人工智能初创公司技术的分销。

作为协议的一部分，微软 Azure 客户将能够使用 Mistral 位于法国的数据中心开发软件，从而为微软在欧洲和受监管行业提供更多容量，以替代美国控制的基础设施。

观看更多为什么 Anthropic 最强大的人工智能模型重新上线

与此同时，Mistral 已将其名为 Medium 3.5 和 OCR 4 的人工智能模型添加到微软名为 Foundry 的应用程序构建器中。 Microsoft Copilot Studio 也推出了 ⁠Medium 3.5。

最后，拥有独立数据中心并通过 Azure Local 访问微软服务的企业将可以选择运行 Mistral 的“开放”模型，该模型允许客户自行开发人工智能。

该协议凸显了欧洲和其他地区越来越有兴趣减少对美国技术的依赖，以便其他国家在未来的社会和经济中拥有更大的发言权。 它还可能帮助微软满足对开放模型不断增长的需求。

了解更多欧盟命令谷歌共享搜索数据并向人工智能竞争对手开放Android系统

尽管推动“主权”人工智能已经有很多年了，但美国上个月决定暂停外国对总部位于旧金山的 Anthropic 的两种先进模型的访问，这使得技术独立成为欧洲更加紧迫的问题。

微软总裁 Brad Smith 和 Mistral 首席执行官 Arthur Mensch 在接受路透社联合采访时表示，此次合作旨在提供此类主权，同时允许访问美国软件和安全功能。

“通过将 Mistral 的模型放在 Azure Local 和 Mistral 的计算能力上，我们可以结合美国和欧洲的技术，并以提供持续和有保障的访问的方式做到这一点，”史密斯说。

据媒体报道，Claude 聊天机器人背后的人工智能实验室也在就一项价值数十亿美元的交易进行谈判，以使用 Facebook 和 Instagram 母公司 Meta 的基础设施。

留在人工智能竞赛中

让欧洲与美国技术脱钩将是一项艰巨的任务。 为全球人工智能热潮提供动力的英伟达芯片也是米斯特拉尔数据中心建设的关键，都是美国设计的。 与微软一样，英伟达也是米斯特拉尔投资者。

观看更多欧洲能否掌握自己的数字命运？ 欧盟公布技术主权路线图

史密斯表示，周二宣布的交易不包括该初创公司的任何新的财务股权，门施拒绝对彭博新闻社的报道发表评论，该报道称米斯特拉尔正在谈判以 200 亿欧元的估值筹集约 30 亿欧元（合 34 亿美元）。

要显示来自 YouTube 的此内容，您必须启用广告跟踪和受众测量。

您的浏览器扩展之一似乎阻止了视频播放器加载。 要观看此内容，您可能需要在此网站上禁用它。

这家位于巴黎的实验室已成为欧洲人工智能领域最大的希望之一。 到目前为止，它的目标是制造业、金融服务和国防销售，并赢得了法国武装部队的业务。 与 Anthropic 等美国同行相比，其估值仍然相形见绌。

尽管如此，门施表示，这笔交易表明微软和米斯特拉尔如何“共同努力缩小欧洲基础设施方面的差距”。

Mistral 的目标是到 2030 年实现 1 吉瓦的计算能力，而微软协议（Mensch 拒绝提供的具体细节）验证了其战略。

他们补充说，两家公司正在制定一项联合上市计划。

“毫无疑问，这将有助于我们两家公司发展业务，”史密斯说。

（法国 24 小时电视台、路透社和法新社）
